sessions.lever: Three combined structural changes drop floor 13->7: (1) init order `mask_const, base, one` flips global-allocator priority so one->$s4 and base->$s5 matching target (retires 4 $20<->$21 substs); (2) outer structure `i=0; if(bits==0) goto reload; tbl=base;` restores the missing 2nd beqz $s1 (retires the insert beq @19); (3) error-loop `p[i<<2]` flips addu dest from $v0 to $v1. All pure C, cheat-reviewer PASS.
sessions.lever_rule: register-alloc-pure-c (allocator priority via assignment order + outer-structure restructure)

sessions.lever: control-flow restructure reproduces the missing beq $s1,$0 branch (96->97 insns); register-rotation $s4/$s5 + $v0/$v1 did NOT yield to decl-order

reviewer.evidence: Layer-1 cheat-reviewer (this session) reviewed candidate.c's three edits: init order, outer restructure, p[i<<2]. PASS on all three. Init-order is analogous to SOTN-sanctioned named-intermediate declaration-order (real assignments all consumed, no dead computation). Outer restructure correctly positions i=0 before the branch so it lands in the delay slot. p[i<<2] is idiomatic C, semantically identical to *(s32*)((u8*)p+(i<<4)).

remaining_gap.summary: Floor 7. Two clusters remain: (a) INIT EMISSION ORDER (~2 Levenshtein ops): scheduler emits `mask, base, one` but target emits `one, mask, base`; RA is now correct (one->$s4, mask->$s3, base->$s5) -- only the prologue issue order differs. Sched1's `longest-critical-path-first` heuristic prefers the lui+ori/lui+addiu chains over the standalone `addiu $s4, 0, 1`. (b) DONE-BLOCK v0/v1 SWAP (~5 substs): val goes to $v1, target wants $v0; pseudo 94 (val, /v global) conflicts with hardreg 2 because local-alloc gives $v0 to pseudo 96 (const 0xFF000000) first during val's live range. Split-val variants drop scheduler nops (95-insn anomaly). The const pseudo's local-alloc grab of $v0 is the blocker; needs more aggressive RA lever or instrumented cc1 (BB2_ALLOC_DEBUG / PRIODBG).
remaining_gap.rules_required_for_full_match: After applying candidate.c the residual gap is encoded in the EXISTING regfix rules (oracle still byte-matches). The 4 $s4/$s5 substs + the insert-beq + the addu operand-reorder are retired by candidate.c. The 5 $v0/$v1 substs in done block + the and operand-reorder + the prologue scheduling diff are the remaining 7.

next_hypotheses: Done block v0/v1: try splitting val into single-write locals while preserving load-delay nops; previous attempts dropped to 95 insns due to scheduler fusion. Try with explicit __volatile__ load barrier between them, or a memory barrier between the load and the use (no -- memory barrier is a cheat). Better: instrumented cc1 PRIODBG to read the local-alloc decision for pseudo 96.

Init prologue scheduling order: try to inflate one's apparent critical path so sched1 schedules it first. E.g. make one's consumer (the sllv) appear LATER in dependence chain, or shorten the mask/base chains' apparent length. Hard -- both chains are 2-insn lui+_ already.
Try the original target source structure -- maybe the original C had `s32 *base = &D_800A2640[0];` or some other base-init form that GCC compiles differently.

rejected_forms.form: swap declaration order of base/one (s32 one; s32 *base; vs s32 *base; s32 one;)
rejected_forms.reason: Pseudo numbers shuffle but RA priority is intrinsic to variables, not pseudo numbers. Same emitted asm.

rejected_forms.form: decl orders: one_first, all_scalars_first, register_one (8 variants)
rejected_forms.reason: Decl order doesn't change emitted asm once we have the right outer structure + init order. All produced floor 9.

rejected_forms.form: while-loop restructure (a_separate_checks) + goto outer recheck (b) -- from prior session
rejected_forms.reason: Reproduces the missing branch (97 insns) but the RA swap costs +1 over HEAD floor. Floor improvement was elusive until init-order lever found.

rejected_forms.form: i += one (replacing i++ inside inner loop)
rejected_forms.reason: Lowers score by fixing RA but emits `addu s0,s0,s4` vs target's `addiu s0,s0,1` -- semantically the same but different opcode. Borderline cheat-by-spelling (using `one` solely to influence RA in an i++ context). REJECTED as cheat.
Same family as i += one. Cheat-by-spelling -- using `one` for non-mask-formation purpose solely to influence RA.
Adds dependency on `one` but breaks the lui+ori emission of mask_const.

rejected_forms.form: inline_base_reread: drop base var, use D_800A2640 directly in tbl assignment
rejected_forms.reason: Inserts lui+addiu inside outer per iteration (breaks LICM hoisting). 94 insns vs target 97.

rejected_forms.form: split val into two_val_short / first_val / val0_val1 / fresh_locals
rejected_forms.reason: Scheduler fuses load-delay nops into delay slots since the two vals are independent. Produces 95 insns vs target 97.

rejected_forms.form: compound_andne / via_xor / via_subtract / complement in done block
rejected_forms.reason: Changes the AND-compare structure. complement (~val&0x7F000000) lowers masked score to 4 but adds a `nor` insn (98 vs 97). Won't byte-match.

rejected_forms.form: register hint on val / ctrl / one
rejected_forms.reason: No effect on RA priority (already at floor 9 baseline, but doesn't push lower).

rejected_forms.form: volatile_ptr_negative: declare ctrl as volatile s32 *
rejected_forms.reason: No floor improvement at this baseline.
